_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Determine level of assurance of developed capabilities based on test results.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with clear criteria based on organizational maturity, team capability, and long-term operational cost.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-006 (LS, cosine: 0.521), OP-K-008 (OP, cosine: 0.502).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.4/10 (raw 0.235). Shared vocabulary: [based]. DCWF-only terms [assurance, capabilities, determine, developed, level, results] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, build, buy, capability, clear, components] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.876, R=0.863). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (90.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Determine level of assurance of developed capabilities based on test results." and "Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with c". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.528 > STS 0.235 (gap +0.293). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [based] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Shared tokens: [based]. DCWF-only: [assurance, capabilities, determine, developed, level, results]. WIDAI-only: [ability, build, buy, capability, clear, components].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 90.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.293 with low STS 0.235. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
